caption.text:
🔭 What international legal frameworks apply to digital conflict and digital harms in conflict settings? Where are the gaps, and what are the options for filling them? 

💡 Join us live as full_name_user_0002, full_name_user_0003 of the full_name_user_0004 Center at UC full_name_user_0005 of Law, presents research on the international legal architecture for digital peacemaking, followed by Q&A with both in-person and online participants.

📢 Is your organisation exposed to hate speech online?

Together with a consortium of organizations, we are kick-starting a mentoring programme for civil society organisations and media outlets that are experiencing hate speech or discriminatory discourse on (their) social media.

The organisations will receive:
✅ A personalised analysis of what is happening on your channels and on boarding to Phoenix, our social media analysis tool
✅ Practical recommendations on how to respond and prevent it effectively
✅ Support to implement concrete and tailored interventions 

Any questions? Email us at email_0001

📝 Sign up via the link in bio.

full_name_user_0021 is partnering with username_user_0020 on the upcoming publication of the 31st issue of Accord.

🔎 Accord 31 will focus on how peacemakers can effectively integrate digital communications, data technologies and AI into peacemaking policies, strategies, legal frameworks, practice and capabilities. 

🗣 In this 90 minute open meeting (join for as long as you can), we will discuss both your experiences with and questions about technology and peacemaking. 

💡 Join us to preview core themes and early findings on digital uplift in peacebuilding organizations, and explore together what integration looks like in practice, what challenges organizations are facing, and what the publication can offer to move the field forward. 

Register through link in bio 👆🔗.

📡 full_name_user_0023 Report: full_name_user_0024 Narratives and full_name_user_0025 of the 2026 full_name_user_0026

This second analysis in our Bangladesh election series finds that the Bangladeshi online space is increasingly volatile and contested.

📈 full_name_user_0027-December period saw major political events - including the full_name_user_0028 Tribunal (ICT) verdict against full_name_user_0029, the assassination of full_name_user_0030 Hadi, and the death of full_name_user_0031 - fuelling spikes in harmful digital narratives.

Read the full report for key insights and implications on the upcoming elections (link in bio👆).

What do Sudanese people think must be in place for a negotiation process to lead to a binding and sustainable ceasefire? 

full_name_user_0033, we asked over 10,000 Sudanese people, and 2,110 answered. 

Here is what they had to say.

Our 2024 Year in Review is here 🚀 (yes, 2024) -- a little later than planned, but full of insights that still resonate as we wrap up 2025. 

✨ It’s a chance to pause, look back, and reflect on what we learned, what we’re carrying forward, and what continues to inspire us.

Read the full 2024 full_name_user_0036 (link in bio 🔗) to explore the highlights and lessons that shaped our work this year — and stay tuned for the 2025 edition, arriving right on schedule this time. 🙈

Yesterday, FECoMo joined partners for the launch of the full_name_user_0039 full_name_user_0040, a session organised by username_user_0038 howtobuildup following the training of the AI full_name_user_0041 model.

This conversation builds on our participation in the full_name_user_0042 Conference 2025 in Barcelona, where FECoMo members helped present Kenya’s progress in developing a context-grounded AI/ML model for identifying affective polarization. Seeing this work now shared locally is a powerful reminder of the value of participatory research and collaboration.

The convening brought together annotators, researchers, policymakers, and digital rights actors to reflect on the findings, share insights from the annotation process, and explore next steps in addressing online polarization in Kenya.

FECoMo remains committed to strengthening safer, inclusive, and rights-based digital spaces for all. 🇰🇪✨

  #KenyaPolarizationFootprint 
#SocialMediaForPeace #AIPeaceClassifier

A #BuildPeace2025 també vam obrir espais per escoltar experiències personals que ens ajuden a entendre millor els impactes dels conflictes i les formes de resistència que neixen a cada territori.

En aquest vídeo escoltem les veus de full_name_user_0050 (full_name_user_0051) i full_name_user_0052 (Centre for full_name_user_0053), que comparteixen reflexions sobre memòria, identitat i esperança des de realitats molt diferents.

Uns testimonis breus però potents que recullen molt bé l’essència de la conferència: diversitat de veus, aprenentatge mutu i creativitat al servei de la pau.

caption.text:
La #BuildPeace2025 ha permès posar en contacte organitzacions locals i internacionals, i una àmplia varietat d'actors de diferents àmbits: de l'activisme, l'acadèmia i el món tecnològic.

Un espai de networking i aprenentatges molt enriquidor!

#peacebuilding #construcciódepau

caption.text:
❗Online polarization is both a digital harm that drives conflict and a negative externality of the attention economy. 

💡 The polarization footprint is our answer for how to measure the systemic risks online platforms pose to peace, and how conflict mitigation can be incorporated in our approach to technology governance.

🗣 Join us for this webinar to learn about our methodology to measure polarization footprints, results of the Kenya polarization footprint, and reflections on how this can all inform a conflict sensitive approach to regulation.

Register through the link in bio.

Avui fa una setmana de l’inici de #BuildPeace2025, la conferència internacional que vam acollir a full_name_user_0068 juntament amb full_name_user_0021.

Durant tres dies, participants d’arreu del món van abordar temes com la memòria, la polarització, la tecnologia i el paper de les comunitats en la construcció de pau.

Aquest vídeo recull alguns moments destacats i l’ambient de treball col·lectiu que va marcar la trobada.

***

One week ago, #BuildPeace2025 kicked off:  the international conference we hosted at full_name_user_0068 together with full_name_user_0021.

Over three days, participants from around the world explored themes such as memory, polarisation, technology, and the role of communities in building peace.

This video offers a look at the collaborative atmosphere and key dynamics that shaped the gathering.

caption.text:
El tercer i últim dia de la #BuildPeace2025 ha posat el focus en com les societats gestionen l’oblit i la memòria, especialment en contextos de violència, autoritarisme i conflictes. 

Els debats han evidenciat fins a quin punt les narratives públiques, també les digitals, poden fragmentar-se, simplificar-se o instrumentalitzar-se, i com això condiciona els processos de reconeixement, reparació i convivència.

S’han compartit experiències que van des de l’ús de plataformes col·laboratives com la Viquipèdia fins a projectes de memòria digital en contextos de guerra, passant per pràctiques comunitàries que reivindiquen la veu de dones sovint en situació de vulnerabilitat. També s’ha subratllat el paper de l’escolta profunda per interrompre el cercle entre trauma i polarització, i la importància de preservar les històries del passat per evitar relats únics que invisibilitzen vides i resistències.

La conferència s'ha retransmès a través de la full_name_user_0042 Radio, liderada per full_name_user_0073 username_user_0070, que ha sortit al carrer per endinsar-se als carrers de full_name_user_0074 i presenciar el mural col·laboratiu a càrrec de l'artista username_user_0071 

Després de tres dies intensos d'activitat, la #BuildPeace2025 s'ha tancat amb un anunci final molt esperat: l'edició de 2026 tindrà lloc a la ciutat de Waterloo, al Canadà!

📸 username_user_0072

#peacebuilding
